_publications._publicationTitle: Patient-Derived Tumor Organoid Rings for Histologic Characterization and High-Throughput Screening

_contextSnippet: Always make sure to close the ring by completing a full circle around the well while releasing the cell/Matrigel mixture. If all the mixture is released before you complete the circle, hold the pipette piston on the first resistance and complete the ring.
details: Two common ring formation defects and their solutions: (1) Open/partial rings — complete a full circle around the well rim; if mixture runs out before the circle closes, hold the pipette on first resistance and finish the arc; (2) Air bubbles — stop pipetting at the first resistance of the pipette even if not all Matrigel is released; discard affected mini-rings or aspirate bubbles from maxi-rings before gelation
